items.raw_claim: LWN reports that AI scrapers now heavily use residential proxies, defeating IP-based blocking, and describes its production countermeasures.
items.summary: LWN published an operational update on AI-scraper crawl load: abusive bots now route through residential proxy networks, making IP-based blocking largely useless, and LWN details the countermeasures it is running in production. Directly relevant to anyone operating a public site — hostile crawl traffic increasingly looks like ordinary home users. Read it to calibrate your own rate-limiting and bot-defense strategy.
items.title: An update on residential proxies and the scraper situation
items.uncertainty: Countermeasure effectiveness is specific to LWN's traffic profile and may not transfer directly.
items.verdict_reason: High-signal, production-proven security/infrastructure report broadly useful to solo builders.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: First-hand operational report on a risk that affects every public-facing site a solo dev runs.

items.raw_claim: pgrust, a Postgres rewrite in Rust, claims to pass 100% of the Postgres regression tests.
items.summary: pgrust is a rewrite of Postgres in Rust that the author says now passes the entire Postgres regression suite. If the claim holds it is a remarkable open-source milestone, though regression-test parity is far from production readiness (performance, replication, extensions, ecosystem). Nothing to act on now; watch for independent verification and benchmarks.
items.title: Postgres rewritten in Rust, now passing 100% of the Postgres regression tests
items.uncertainty: The 100% regression-suite claim is the author's own and has not been independently verified; production viability unknown.
items.verdict_reason: Interesting but unverified author claim; no near-term action, narrower audience.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Potentially significant OSS milestone for anyone running Postgres, which includes Fuzzy's Hetzner stack.

items.raw_claim: A developer converted his Android app into a plain webpage and reports the app was unnecessary for his use case.
items.summary: Dan Q converted his Android app into a plain webpage and documents what was gained and lost, arguing most apps could have been webpages. A concrete, first-hand datapoint for solo builders weighing native app delivery (store fees, review friction, platform lock-in) against web reach. Useful framing when deciding whether a new product needs a native client at all.
items.title: I converted an Android app to a webpage
items.uncertainty: Single anecdote; conclusions may not transfer to apps needing push, background, or offline-heavy features.
items.verdict_reason: Useful builder report, but single-anecdote scope suits private digest.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Relevant to native-vs-web delivery decisions, including CalenCall-style iOS product choices.

items.raw_claim: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI alleging trade-secret theft by former Apple employees.
items.summary: Apple has reportedly filed suit against OpenAI, accusing former Apple employees of taking trade secrets. Reporting is early and secondhand (9to5mac citing MacRumors); no court filings analyzed yet. No direct action for solo devs, but sustained legal pressure on OpenAI is platform risk worth tracking if you build on their APIs.
items.title: Apple sues OpenAI, accuses ex-employees of stealing trade secrets
items.uncertainty: Secondhand reporting; scope of the claims and any product impact are unknown until filings are public.
items.verdict_reason: Early-stage legal news; track for API/platform fallout, no action yet.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: Platform-risk signal for anyone with OpenAI API dependencies.

items.raw_claim: A hobbyist built a smart fan controlled over iroh's p2p networking stack without any cloud service.
items.summary: A builder wired a smart fan using iroh, the Rust p2p networking library, demonstrating direct device-to-device control with no cloud broker. Toy project, but a clean demo of iroh's leverage for local-first tooling — a plausible alternative transport for agent/device communication without hosting a relay.
items.title: An iroh powered smart fan
items.uncertainty: Vendor blog showcasing its own library; real-world reliability of iroh at scale not addressed.
items.verdict_reason: Toy demo, but signals iroh maturing as p2p infrastructure — track, no action.
items.worth_mentioning_reason: iroh is worth tracking as p2p plumbing for local-first and agent-to-agent work.

items.raw_claim: Stratechery's weekly digest for the week of July 6, 2026 covers Microsoft potentially pulling the plug on Xbox hardware.
items.summary: Stratechery's weekly recap email pointing to the week's paywalled essays, headlined by analysis of Microsoft's Xbox strategy. It is a roundup wrapper with no standalone claim or action for a solo builder.
items.title: 2026.28: XBOX On the Rocks

items.worth_mentioning_reason: Stratechery's weekly recap email pointing to the week's paywalled essays, headlined by analysis of Microsoft's Xbox strategy. It is a roundup wrapper with no standalone claim or action for a solo builder.

# Morning memo — 2026-07-12

**Source failures:** none detected in the last fetch cycle.

## Worth attention

- **An update on residential proxies and the scraper situation** — https://lwn.net/SubscriberLink/1080822/990a8a5e2d379085/
  LWN published an operational update on AI-scraper crawl load: abusive bots now route through residential proxy networks, making IP-based blocking largely useless, and LWN details the countermeasures it is running. If you operate any public site, expect hostile crawl traffic to look like ordinary home users. Worth reading to calibrate your own rate-limiting and bot-defense posture.

- **Postgres rewritten in Rust, now passing 100% of the Postgres regression tests** — https://github.com/malisper/pgrust
  pgrust is a rewrite of Postgres in Rust that the author says passes the entire Postgres regression suite. If the claim holds it is a notable open-source milestone, though regression-test parity is far from production readiness. Nothing to act on now; watch for independent verification.

- **I converted an Android app to a webpage** — https://danq.me/2026/07/09/your-app-could-have-been-a-webpage/
  A developer converted his Android app into a plain webpage and walks through what was gained and lost, arguing most apps could be webpages. Concrete datapoint for solo builders weighing native app vs. web delivery.

## Monitoring

- **An iroh powered smart fan** — https://www.iroh.computer/blog/an-iroh-powered-smart-fan — toy project, but a clean demo of iroh p2p as a cloudless transport for device/agent communication.
